name: Tavily Search API
what_it_does: Tavily is a search API built specifically for AI agents and LLM applications. Unlike general-purpose search APIs, Tavily returns clean, structured results optimized for LLM context injection — full article content, not just snippets. It handles query optimization, result filtering, and context compression internally. Supports both search and topic-specific retrieval (news, general). Has an official LangChain integration and MCP server.
use_cases: Web research in ReAct-style agents (search → reason → search loop)
Grounding LLM responses with current web information
News monitoring and event tracking for agents
Building RAG systems that need fresh web content
Replacing manual browsing in research workflows
not_for: Private/intranet content that isn't indexed publicly
best_when: Agent needs to find and retrieve current web information as part of a reasoning loop — Tavily returns ready-to-use content, not just links.
avoid_when: You need to scrape a specific known URL — use Firecrawl or Jina Reader instead.

alternatives.reason: Firecrawl is better when you have a specific URL to read; Tavily is better for open-ended search queries
Jina Reader is free for URL-to-markdown; Tavily wins for query-based search with relevance ranking

auth.notes: Single API key. No scopes. Pass as tvly-api-key header or in request body. Very simple — designed for quick agent integration.

pricing.paid_tiers: Researcher: $35/month (5,000 credits), Developer: $99/month (20,000 credits), Business: custom. $0.035-0.007 per search at scale.
pricing.notes: Credits are consumed per search. Results depth setting (basic vs advanced) affects credit consumption. Very competitive pricing for AI agent use cases.

performance.rate_limits: Free: 10 req/min; Paid: 60+ req/min depending on tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors are structured JSON with message field. Credit exhaustion and rate limit errors are clearly communicated. Search quality errors (no results) are graceful.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search is deterministic per query (though results change as web changes). Safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Results are time-sensitive — the same query returns different results as the web changes, so don't cache for long
include_raw_content: true is expensive in tokens — use sparingly in context-limited agents
max_results defaults to 5; increasing to 10+ increases token cost significantly
Search topic 'news' returns recent articles but may miss older authoritative sources
No way to filter by date range — all results are ranked by Tavily's internal relevance
Rate limits are per API key, so shared keys in multi-agent systems deplete quickly
